_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Deviated nasal septum resulting in Nasal obstruction
Deviated nasal septum resulting in Recurrent epistaxis
As an initial surgery to create adequate nasal space to perform endoscopic nasal surgeries

preauth.label: Clinical notes (detailing signs, symptoms, examination findings, indications for doing the procedure, circumstances of the incident which led to Deviated Nasal Septum& advise for admission)
Anterior rhinoscopy/ endoscopic picture showing deviated nasal septum

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes (including clinical signs & symptoms such as difficulty in breathing, examination findings, indications for doing the procedure & advice for admission)?
Anterior rhinoscopy/ endoscopic picture showing deviated nasal septum?

cpd_claim.q: Do OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and outcomes of the surgery?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advice including for follow-up?

common_queries: Clinical notes are incomplete or do not provide sufficient detail regarding signs, symptoms, examination findings, indications, or admission advice for Septoplasty.
The mandatory anterior rhinoscopy or endoscopic picture showing deviated nasal septum is missing or inconclusive.
Patient's age is below the recommended minimum (14-16 years) for Septoplasty without specific clinical justification for limited intervention.
Operative notes are missing or lack sufficient detail on the surgical steps performed and the outcomes of the Septoplasty procedure.
Documents demonstrating appropriate post-operative care, follow-up advice, or a detailed discharge summary are missing or incomplete.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, and examination.
Septoplasty is a surgical correction of defects and deformities of nasal septum causing nasal obstruction or recurrent epistaxis.
contraindications: Surgery should not be planned in children before completion of the development of facial skeleton (at least 14 to 16 years of age).
In case of severe symptoms in pediatric patients which is not controlled by medical management, limited septoplasty should be considered avoiding the growth centers in the nasal septum.
